originalTitle: UK borrows less than expected in June but public finances remain a challenge
summary: Public finances for last month were better than expected but the UK is carrying major debt.
content:
The UK's new chancellor John Healey

The government borrowed slightly less than expected in June, according to figures published as new Prime Minister Andy Burnham began setting out measures to cut living costs for households.

Borrowing - the difference between spending and income from taxes - was £16bn, about £7.9bn lower than a year earlier, although analysts said challenges remained over the UK's public finances.

Other data showed the unemployment rate was unchanged, with the Office for National Statistics (ONS) saying the labour market was "relatively steady".

However, while the borrowing figure was less than forecast, the ONS said total debt was high by historical standards and close to the annual value of the UK economy.

Borrowing for June was slightly below the £16.3bn that had been predicted by the government's official forecaster, the Office for Budget Responsibility (OBR).

Ruth Gregory, deputy chief UK economist at Capital Economics, said June's figure was "a rare piece of good news" for the new prime minister and his new Chancellor, John Healey.

However, she added: "Overall, there's no escaping the fact that the public finances are fragile and that there is limited scope for extra borrowing."

So far in the current financial year, borrowing has reached a total of £57.6bn, according to the Office for National Statistics (ONS). While this is down £3.7bn from the same period last year, it is £2.7bn above the OBR's forecast.

James Smith, chief UK economist at ING, told the BBC's Today programme the fact that borrowing was still running ahead of the OBR's projections was "a reminder of the challenges that the new chancellor and the new prime minister face".

He added they would face a "difficult picture" at the autumn Budget, with "lots of tough choices to be made".

Burnham and Healey have both pledged to stick to former chancellor Rachel Reeves' fiscal rules on spending and borrowing, although the new prime minister said on Monday he would use "any flexibility within them" to help with policy changes.

Shortly after Burnham's comments were made public, the yield on 10-year government bonds - effectively the interest rate charged to the UK government for a 10-year loan - rose above 5%.

The yield hit 5.03% late on Monday afternoon. However, when trading began on Tuesday the rate slipped back slightly to 5.01%.

In a statement released on Monday, Healey said "fiscal credibility is the bedrock for economic stability and for national security".

Shadow chancellor Sir Mel Stride said Labour had "maxed out the nation's credit card and sent borrowing soaring".

"If Andy Burnham and John Healey are really serious about the future of our economy then they must get borrowing under control," he said.

The new leadership's first major policy announcement was to cut VAT on household electricity bills from 5% to zero from the beginning of October.

Ministers said it would be funded by savings from the cancellation of the digital ID programme, but Labour's Darren Jones, who was sacked as chief secretary to the prime minister on Monday, accused the government of announcing an unfunded tax cut.

VAT to be cut from household electricity bills in October

Burnham promises help with your money. How could it affect you?

How much money does the UK government borrow, and why does it matter?

June's borrowing figure was helped by higher revenues from income tax and VAT, while interest payments on inflation-linked debt fell.

The government paid £11.8bn in debt interest payments in June, which was nearly a third lower than the same point last year.

However, it was still the fourth highest total for June on record, the ONS said.

Public sector net debt currently stands at nearly £3 trillion, which is almost as much as the value of all the goods and services produced in the UK in a year, known as the gross domestic product, or GDP.

However, in the October 2024 Budget, Reeves changed the definition of debt that the government would use in its fiscal rules to a broader measure called public sector net financial liabilities (PSNFL).

Under this measure, total debt was £2.7tn at the end of June 2026, the equivalent of 84.5% of GDP.

Meanwhile, the latest survey of the labour market showed the unemployment rate remained unchanged at 4.9%.

Growth in regular earnings - which excludes bonuses - remained unchanged, rising at an annual pace of 3.4% in the March to May period.

However, the ONS noted that regular wage growth in the private sector fell below 3% for the first time since 2020.

Yael Selfin, chief economist at KPMG, said the "subdued" wage growth made it more likely that the Bank of England would keep interest rates on hold at 3.75% when it meets next week.

"Weak hiring activity is continuing to weigh on workers' bargaining power, limiting upward pressure on wages," she added.

"Workers are also set to see a renewed squeeze on living standards during the second half of the year as higher energy costs feed through to household bills."
chineseTitle: 英国六月借款低于预期但公共财政仍是挑战
chineseSummary: 上个月的公共财政好于预期，但英国背负着巨额债务。
chineseContent:
英国新任财政大臣约翰·希利

新任总理安迪·伯纳姆开始制定削减家庭生活成本的措施后公布的数据显示，6 月份政府借款略低于预期。

尽管分析师表示英国公共财政仍面临挑战，但借款（支出与税收收入之间的差额）为 160 亿英镑，比去年同期减少约 79 亿英镑。

其他数据显示失业率没有变化，英国国家统计局（ONS）表示劳动力市场“相对稳定”。

然而，尽管借款数字低于预期，但英国国家统计局表示，按照历史标准衡量，总债务很高，接近英国经济的年值。

6 月份借款略低于政府官方预测机构预算责任办公室 (OBR) 预测的 163 亿英镑。

凯投宏观(Capital Economics)英国副首席经济学家露丝·格雷戈里(Ruth Gregory)表示，6月的数据对于新首相和新任财政大臣约翰·希利来说是“难得的好消息”。

不过，她补充道：“总体而言，公共财政脆弱且额外借款的空间有限，这是无法回避的事实。”

根据英国国家统计局 (ONS) 的数据，本财年迄今为止，借款总额已达到 576 亿英镑。虽然比去年同期减少了 37 亿英镑，但比 OBR 的预测高出 27 亿英镑。

ING 首席英国经济学家詹姆斯·史密斯 (James Smith) 在 BBC 的《今日》节目中表示，借贷仍然超出 OBR 的预测，这一事实“提醒人们新财政大臣和新首相面临的挑战”。

他补充说，他们将在秋季预算中面临“困难的局面”，“需要做出很多艰难的选择”。

伯纳姆和希利都承诺遵守前总理雷切尔·里夫斯关于支出和借贷的财政规则，尽管新总理周一表示，他将利用“其中的任何灵活性”来帮助政策改变。

伯纳姆的言论公开后不久，10 年期政府债券的收益率（实际上是向英国政府收取的 10 年期贷款利率）升至 5% 以上。

周一下午晚些时候，收益率触及5.03%。然而，当周二交易开始时，利率小幅回落至 5.01%。

希利在周一发布的一份声明中表示，“财政信誉是经济稳定和国家安全的基石”。

影子财政大臣梅尔·斯特莱德爵士表示，工党“已经用尽了国家的信用卡，导致借贷飙升”。

“如果安迪·伯纳姆和约翰·希利真的认真对待我们经济的未来，那么他们必须控制借贷，”他说。

新领导层宣布的第一个重大政策是从10月初起将家庭电费增值税从5%降至零。

部长们表示，该项目的资金将来自于取消数字身份证计划所节省的资金，但周一被解除首相首席秘书职务的工党议员达伦·琼斯(Darren Jones)指责政府宣布了一项没有资金支持的减税计划。

10月份家庭电费将减免增值税

伯纳姆承诺为您提供资金帮助。它对你有何影响？

英国政府借了多少钱？为什么这很重要？

6月份的借贷数字得益于所得税和增值税收入的增加，而与通胀挂钩的债务的利息支付则有所下降。

6月份政府支付了118亿英镑的债务利息，比去年同期减少了近三分之一。

然而，英国国家统计局表示，这仍然是有记录以来 6 月份第四高的总数。

公共部门净债务目前接近 3 万亿英镑，几乎相当于英国一年内生产的所有商品和服务的价值，即国内生产总值 (GDP)。

然而，在 2024 年 10 月的预算中，里夫斯将政府在其财政规则中使用的债务定义更改为更广泛的衡量标准，称为公共部门净金融负债 (PSNFL)。

根据这一衡量标准，截至 2026 年 6 月末，债务总额为 2.7 兆英镑，相当于 GDP 的 84.5%。

与此同时，最新的劳动力市场调查显示，失业率维持在4.9%不变。

常规收入（不包括奖金）增长保持不变，3 月至 5 月期间年增长率为 3.4%。

然而，英国国家统计局指出，私营部门的常规工资增长率自 2020 年以来首次低于 3%。

毕马威(KPMG)首席经济学家耶尔·塞尔芬(Yael Selfin)表示，“疲软”的工资增长使得英国央行更有可能在下周的会议上将利率维持在3.75%。

她补充说：“招聘活动疲软继续影响工人的议价能力，限制了工资上涨的压力。”

“随着能源成本上升导致家庭账单增加，工人们的生活水平在今年下半年也将再次受到挤压。”
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于全球新闻信号，适合用于观察外部环境、政策变化、地缘风险和市场情绪。
